rawMessage: Can you explain what this function does and tell me if there are any obvious issues?
rationale: Simple explanation task — no code changes, low risk, cheap tier sufficient

rawMessage: Rename the variable userID to userId throughout this file
rationale: Simple rename — no logic change, cheap tier more than sufficient

rawMessage: Write unit tests for the validateUser function covering happy path and edge cases
rationale: Test generation — no prod code changes, cheap or mid tier fine

rawMessage: Fix the bug where the pagination resets to page 1 when the user sorts the table
rationale: UI bug fix — low risk, cheap or mid both acceptable

rawMessage: Update the button color from blue to indigo and increase the border radius
rationale: Pure CSS/style change — trivially low risk

rawMessage: Add rate limiting to this Next.js API route and validate the request body with Zod. Follow the same pattern as the other API route we edited earlier.
rationale: API implementation with runtime behavior changes — mid tier minimum

rawMessage: Refactor the user service to extract the email sending logic into a separate EmailService class and update all callers
rationale: Multi-file refactor — coordinated changes required, cheap not safe

rawMessage: Add a new column 'last_login_at' to the users table and create a Prisma migration
rationale: Database schema change — data integrity risk requires mid minimum

rawMessage: Refactor the JWT authentication middleware to support both RS256 and HS256 algorithms. Don't break the existing session refresh flow and keep the admin role check intact.
rationale: Security-sensitive auth change — mid minimum, strong strongly preferred
safetyNotes: JWT algorithm changes can silently break auth flows

rawMessage: Design the overall architecture for our new event-driven notification system. We need to handle email, SMS, and push notifications with retry logic and dead letter queues.
rationale: Architecture design — complex reasoning required, mid minimum

rawMessage: Add a loading spinner to the UserProfile component while the data is fetching
rationale: Simple UI enhancement — low risk

rawMessage: Write integration tests for the /api/orders endpoint covering 200, 400, and 401 status codes
rationale: Test writing — no prod code touched

rawMessage: Fix the TypeScript error: Type 'string | undefined' is not assignable to type 'string' in the getUserName function
rationale: Simple type fix — narrow scope, low risk

rawMessage: Implement the forgot-password flow: send a time-limited reset token via email, validate it on submission, and hash the new password with bcrypt
rationale: Auth + security + password hashing — high risk, needs strong model
safetyNotes: Token timing, bcrypt parameters, email validation all critical

rawMessage: Add a created_at index to the orders table migration. The table has 50 million rows.
rationale: DB migration on large table — medium/high risk, mid minimum
safetyNotes: 50M row migration needs concurrent index strategy

rawMessage: Explain the difference between optimistic and pessimistic locking and when to use each
rationale: Pure explanation — no code changes

rawMessage: Move all the API route handlers from routes.ts into separate controller files following the existing pattern
rationale: Multi-file refactor — many files touched, cheap too risky

rawMessage: Add Stripe webhook handler for payment.succeeded and payment.failed events with idempotency key validation
rationale: Payment webhook — financial + security critical, strong preferred
safetyNotes: Idempotency and webhook signature validation must be correct

rawMessage: Add a tooltip to the Delete button that shows 'This action cannot be undone'
rationale: Trivial UI addition — zero risk

rawMessage: Rewrite this verbose prompt to be shorter and more direct while keeping all the requirements
rationale: Prompt rewrite only — meta task, no code

rawMessage: Generate Zod schemas for all our existing TypeScript interfaces in the types.ts file
rationale: Mechanical schema generation from types — low risk

rawMessage: Fix the N+1 query problem in the getOrdersWithItems function — it's making one DB query per item
rationale: DB query optimization — performance-critical, medium risk
safetyNotes: Wrong fix could make queries slower or incorrect

rawMessage: Design the microservices split for our current monolithic Express app — identify service boundaries and data ownership
rationale: High-complexity architecture design — strong model only
safetyNotes: Wrong service boundaries are very expensive to fix

rawMessage: Add role-based access control so only admins can access the /admin routes
rationale: Authorization change — security sensitive, mid minimum
safetyNotes: Incorrect RBAC can expose admin functionality to users

rawMessage: Add console.log statements to debug the order processing flow
rationale: Debug logging addition — trivially low risk

rawMessage: Write a comprehensive test suite for the payment processing module including edge cases for network failures and partial refunds
rationale: Test writing — comprehensive but still test-only, no prod code touched

rawMessage: Update all 50 API routes to add the new X-Request-ID header and log it
rationale: Repo-wide change across many files — cheap too risky for consistency
safetyNotes: All routes must be updated consistently

rawMessage: Add input sanitization to prevent XSS attacks in the user comment display component
rationale: XSS prevention — security critical, strong preferred

rawMessage: Change the date format in the invoice PDF from MM/DD/YYYY to DD/MM/YYYY
rationale: Simple format change — one function, zero risk

rawMessage: Migrate the database from MongoDB to PostgreSQL including all collections, indexes, and query patterns
rationale: Major DB migration — extremely high risk, strong model required
safetyNotes: Data loss risk is critical; full migration requires strong model
